2604.11364.title: The Missing Knowledge Layer in Cognitive Architectures for AI Agents
2604.11364.paper_summary: The two most influential cognitive architecture frameworks for AI agents, CoALA [21] and JEPA [12], both lack an explicit Knowledge layer with its own persistence semantics. This gap produces a category error: systems apply cognitive decay to factual claims, or treat facts and experiences with identical update mechanics. We survey persistence semantics across existing memory systems and identify eight convergence points, from Karpathy's LLM Knowledge Base [10] to the BEAM benchmark's near-zero contradiction-resolution scores [22], all pointing to related architectural gaps. We propose a four-layer decom position (Knowledge, Memory, Wisdom, Intelligence) where each layer has fundamentally different persistence semantics: indefinite supersession, Ebbinghaus decay, evidence-gated revision, and ephemeral inference respectively. Companion implementations in Python and Rust demonstrate the architectural separation is feasible. We borrow terminology from cognitive science as a useful analogy (the Knowledge/Memory distinction echoes Tulving's trichotomy), but our layers are engineering constructs justified by persistence-semantics requirements, not by neural architecture. We argue that these distinctions demand distinct persistence semantics in engineering implementations, and that no current framework or system provides this.
2604.11364.paper_summary_zh: 兩個對AI代理最具影響力的認知架構框架，CoALA [21] 和 JEPA [12]，都缺乏具有自身持久性語義的明確知識層。這一缺口產生了一種類別錯誤：系統對事實聲明應用認知衰退，或將事實和經驗視為具有相同更新機制。我們調查了現有記憶系統中的持久性語義，並確定了八個收斂點，從Karpathy的LLM知識庫 [10] 到BEAM基準的近零矛盾解決分數 [22]，所有這些都指向相關的架構缺口。我們提出了一個四層分解（知識、記憶、智慧、智能），每一層具有根本不同的持久性語義：不確定的取代、艾賓浩斯衰退、證據門控修訂和短暫推理。我們在Python和Rust中的伴隨實現展示了架構分離是可行的。我們借用認知科學的術語作為有用的類比（知識/記憶的區分呼應了Tulving的三分法），但我們的層次是由持久性語義要求所證明的工程構造，而不是由神經架構所決定。我們認為這些區分要求在工程實現中具有不同的持久性語義，而目前沒有任何框架或系統提供這一點。
2604.11364.author: Michaël Roynard et.al.
